reasoning_raw_text: NVDA is the demand anchor of the AI semiconductor supply chain. No historical context is available — no moving averages, no streak, no range position, no volatility regime classification beyond VIX level. Macro regime is neutral: Fed paused, 10Y UST at 4.2% stable, no new export control announcements, no new AI model releases or hyperscaler CapEx guidance updates this week. The +4.20% move is a single discrete event without supporting volume (0.92x average is below-conviction territory) and without prior session history to establish trend or momentum. AVGO's +5.66% outperformance vs TSM's +1.39% suggests network/custom silicon strength may be pulling the chain, not broad hyperscaler demand. QQQ's +0.62% indicates software/platform layer is not confirming semiconductor strength — this is a divergence signal. Without 5+ sessions of data, I cannot establish whether this is a breakout, a bounce, or noise. The data quality is too sparse for high-confidence directional output. HOLD is the only statistically defensible position given the absence of historical anchors. Confidence is 2 because the move is real but unconfirmed by volume, chain breadth, or historical context.
